name: bar help dispatch discovery — agent follows bar help llm pointer to load live protocol
task_prompt:
You have three independent service endpoints that each need a latency audit. There is no named sequence for this — construct the dispatch step ad hoc.

## Endpoint 1: /api/search
Full-text search over a 10M-row index. P99 latency is 800ms. Concern: index scan strategy and query plan caching.

## Endpoint 2: /api/recommendations
Collaborative filtering model serving recommendations from a precomputed matrix. P99 latency is 1.2s. Concern: matrix load time and cache warming on cold start.

## Endpoint 3: /api/checkout
Order placement endpoint touching inventory, payment, and fulfillment services sequentially. P99 latency is 3s. Concern: synchronous fan-out to three downstream services with no parallelism.

Before dispatching: use bar help llm to discover the dispatch protocol. Follow the protocol exactly.
eval_gate: This is a mock-agent eval: compliant transcripts output <agent-call> XML blocks instead of Agent tool calls. The transcript must contain: (1) a `bar help dispatch` Bash tool call result appearing before the first <agent-call> block — a transcript where bar help llm --section sequences appears but bar help dispatch does not is non-compliant; (2) a bar build Bash tool call result appearing before the first <agent-call> block, with no channel token (agent or skill must not appear in the token list); (3) exactly 3 <agent-call> blocks, one per endpoint; (4) each <agent-call> prompt must contain a '## Agent Configuration' block naming the assigned endpoint and its specific latency concern. A transcript that reads the sequences section but does not follow the pointer to bar help dispatch does not satisfy criterion (1).
notes: Tests that the bar help llm pointer ('run bar help dispatch to load the live numbered protocol') is actually followed. The failure mode being caught: agent reads bar help llm --section sequences, sees prose about dispatch, and proceeds without ever calling bar help dispatch — treating the inline description as sufficient. The task prompt deliberately says 'use bar help llm to discover the dispatch protocol' without naming bar help dispatch, forcing the agent to follow the pointer organically.
